Key Innovation: Software-defined NPU with photonic interconnects and TFLN direct Pockels modulation at  $100+$  GHz; uses topology-aware routing and, algorithmic provable mathematical principles.
Energy Efficiency Feature: Replaces electrons with photons for data movement; mathematical scheduling replaces heuristics to reduce GPU idle time and eliminate electrical I/O bottlenecks.
Market or Performance Data: $2.5 \times$  power efficiency vs. GPU;  $233 \times$  throughput-per-watt vs. NVIDIA A100;  $70-85\%$  utilization;  $1.6$  Pbps bisection bandwidth.
Current Stage or Status: Seed Round ( $\$2.8$ M– $\$5$ M);  $\$180$ M LOI pipeline; PCB fabrication phase.

Key Innovation: 3D photonic chip-on-wafer optical interposer allowing vertical chip communication via built-in solid-state optical circuit switching.
Energy Efficiency Feature: Eliminates shoreline bandwidth limitations and copper retimers; 3D Photonic Integration at  $\sim 2.5$  pJ/bit to  $\sim 4.6$  pJ/bit.
Market or Performance Data: $114$  Tbps+ interconnect data rate; supports  $1.5$  kW+ power delivery;  $8-10 \times$  faster than competitors.
Current Stage or Status: Production/Deployment; Partnered with GUC, Synopsys, and Cadence for HVM readiness.

Key Innovation: General-purpose photonic AI accelerator using light for linear algebra; employs Wavelength Division Multiplexing (WDM).
Energy Efficiency Feature: Integrates  $44$  GB on-chip SRAM with  $21$  PB/s bandwidth to eliminate external DRAM/HBM data movement energy.
Market or Performance Data: $95-210 \times$  speedup over H100;  $3,000$  tokens/sec inference on GPT-OSS-120B;  $\sim 60\%$  system utilization.

Energy Efficiency Feature: Optical domain calculations use  $90\%$  less energy than GPUs; processes multiple data sets simultaneously using different light colors.
Market or Performance Data: Up to  $10 \times$  faster than Nvidia A100;  $8 \times$  throughput of a server blade at  $20\%$  of the power.

Key Innovation: $5$ nm TSMC technology integrating two high-bandwidth memories with a processor via CoWoS-S interposer.
Energy Efficiency Feature: Massive on-chip SRAM (hundreds of MBs) to minimize external streaming; conservative  $1$  GHz clock to reduce power draw.
Market or Performance Data: $\sim 40\%$  better performance per watt vs. standard benchmarks;  $2.5 \times$  better performance per watt vs. GPU solutions.

Key Innovation: Systolic array data flow architecture designed to perform large matrix multiplications without external memory access.
Energy Efficiency Feature: Reduces memory traffic by keeping "hot" data on-chip; cuts data movement energy consumption.
Market or Performance Data: Runs at  $150$ W vs.  $350$ W– $1000$ W for high-end GPUs.

Key Innovation: Fully Forward Mode (FFM) learning and hybrid diffraction-interference architecture (shallow but broad width).
Energy Efficiency Feature: Energy efficiency of  $160$  TOPS/W; Taichi-II reported as  $1000 \times$  more energy efficient than NVIDIA H100 GPU.
Market or Performance Data: $880$  trillion MACS/mm²; accelerates training by  $10 \times$ ; supports networks with billions of neurons.
Current Stage or Status: Validated on benchmarks; Published in Science; Research/Debut stage.

Key Innovation: $1.6$ T Silicon Photonics CPO Chip utilizing Micro Ring Modulators (MRM) and a 3D-stacked engine.
Energy Efficiency Feature: $3.5 \times$  power savings by replacing retimed electrical links with co-packaged optics.
Market or Performance Data: $200$  Gb/s per MRM;  $1.6$  Tb/s total throughput.

Key Innovation: Heterogeneous integration pathways for high-volume manufacturing of Gen 2 and Gen 3 CPO-based switches.
Energy Efficiency Feature: $65\%$  CPO power savings compared to traditional retimed optics.
Market or Performance Data: $90\%$  training efficiency improvement for  $24$ K GPU clusters;  $1$ M CPO device hours without link flaps.

Key Innovation: Analog computation using memristors to merge memory and processing (mortal computation).
Energy Efficiency Feature: Eliminates von Neumann bottleneck; operates at  $3$  orders of magnitude less power than traditional accelerators.
Market or Performance Data: Capable of storing  $1,000$  distinct analog levels/states per device.
Current Stage or Status: Startup producing and selling circuits for AI inference.

Key Innovation: Mimics human brain neurons using integer spikes, remaining inactive until significant signals occur.
Energy Efficiency Feature: Reduces energy use by  $97\%$  by skipping approximately  $70\%$  of calculations.
Market or Performance Data: $100 \times$  faster than current LLMs; successfully trained  $76$  billion parameter model.

Key Innovation: Brain-inspired processor combining Spiking Neural Network (SNN) and CNN architectures on a  $3$ mm chip.
Energy Efficiency Feature: Consumes  $500 \times$  less energy than traditional chips; utilizes event-driven "quiet" periods.
Market or Performance Data: $100 \times$  faster than current sensor-targeted chips.

Key Innovation: Very Large Scale Photonics (VLSP) engine integrating hundreds of lasers into a single photonic chip.
Energy Efficiency Feature: Replaces discrete ELSFP modules to overcome the "power wall" of manual assembly.
Market or Performance Data: Powers  $51.2$  Tbps bandwidth; enables  $100$  Tbps switch in 1RU ( $4 \times$  rack space reduction).
Current Stage or Status: Sampling with customers; platforms deployed for evaluation.

Key Innovation: In-package optical I/O chiplets replacing electrical SerDes for high-performance compute clusters.
Energy Efficiency Feature: Replaces electrical I/O with high-bandwidth optical interconnects to significantly reduce power draw.
Market or Performance Data: Supports scale-up fabrics for  $100-10,000$  GPUs; 3rd generation data released Aug '25.

Key Innovation: World's first smartphone computing platform featuring a dedicated hardware NPU.
Energy Efficiency Feature: Architected for mobile efficiency; up to  $50 \times$  more power efficient than traditional CPUs for AI tasks.
Market or Performance Data: Up to  $25 \times$  performance improvement in AI tasks.

Key Innovation: $7$ nm process chip using transistors with two thresholds to distinguish three logic states (trits).
Energy Efficiency Feature: Consumes  $60\%$  less power and uses  $40\%$  fewer components than binary chips.
Market or Performance Data: Operates  $20\%$  faster than traditional binary equivalents.

Key Innovation: Calibration-free Sinkhorn-Normalized Quantization method for Large Language Models.
Energy Efficiency Feature: Cuts LLM memory usage by  $60–70\%$ , reducing the energy footprint of hardware deployments.
Market or Performance Data: Allows  $60$  GB models to run on  $20$  GB hardware (e.g., RTX 4090).

Key Innovation: Stacks 3D NAND dies using HBM advanced packaging for parallel I/O.
Energy Efficiency Feature: $2.69 \times$  better performance per watt compared to HBM-only configurations.
Market or Performance Data: $1,638+$  GB/s bandwidth; up to  $512$  GB capacity.

Key Innovation: Server solution utilizing phase change memories (memristor tech) and Gaudi accelerators.
Energy Efficiency Feature: Integrated photonics and dedicated AI cores designed to reduce thermal walls.
Market or Performance Data: Not in source

Key Innovation: Single-chip integration of signal generation, modulation, and detection using Mach-Zehnder modulators.
Energy Efficiency Feature: Eliminates hundreds of fiber connections; utilizes Peltier coolers for thermal stability.
Market or Performance Data: $1.6$  terabits per second throughput.

Key Innovation: Matrix multiplication performed using photons instead of electricity.
Energy Efficiency Feature: Operates using only  $10\%$  of the power required by traditional silicon chips.
Market or Performance Data: Claims  $50 \times$  the performance of silicon chips.

Key Innovation: Linear transformations and matrix calculations handled entirely in the optical domain.
Energy Efficiency Feature: $30 \times$  higher energy efficiency than standard GPUs.
Market or Performance Data: Reaches  $50 \times$  better performance for specific AI tasks.

Key Innovation: Analog optical computing using light intensities to represent numerical data.
Energy Efficiency Feature: Lower energy requirements due to photons moving with near-zero resistance.
Market or Performance Data: Claims  $100 \times$  speed improvement.
